title: Automating Sub-Element Creation Based on Dropdown Selection

posts.author: Corentin (CPL)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone,
I’m trying to automate my CRM in Monday.com and I need help setting up a specific automation.
What I want to do is whenever an item is selected from a dropdown menu in a column, a sub-element should be automatically created with the name of the selected item.

Does anyone know how to set up this automation?
I would appreciate any help or guidance!
Thank you in advance!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you have Pro or Enterprise? This will help whether you can use automations and/or workflows.

posts.author: Corentin (CPL)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Desiree! I use Monday for the company I work for, so I am guessing it is Enterprise.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you have this logo at the top?

posts.author: Corentin (CPL)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! No, I do not. I am guessing I do not have Enterprise then. However, please know that I only have the “user” role on Monday. I am not an admin.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Even as a user, you’d be able to see it. With it, you’d be able to do what you’re looking for natively. You might also consider the app ‘subitem automations’

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Corentin !

This automation from Subitem Automations is very similar to what you’re trying to achieve. Have you tried this automation before?
